server.c(3) Library Functions Manual server.c(3)

NAME

server.c

SYNOPSIS

Functions


asciichat_error_t tcp_server_init (tcp_server_t *server, const tcp_server_config_t *config)
Initialize TCP server. asciichat_error_t tcp_server_run (tcp_server_t *server)
Run TCP server accept loop. void tcp_server_destroy (tcp_server_t *server)
Shutdown TCP server. void tcp_server_set_cleanup_callback (tcp_server_t *server, tcp_client_cleanup_fn cleanup_fn)
Set client cleanup callback. asciichat_error_t tcp_server_add_client (tcp_server_t *server, socket_t socket, void *client_data)
Add client to registry. asciichat_error_t tcp_server_remove_client (tcp_server_t *server, socket_t socket)
Remove client from registry. asciichat_error_t tcp_server_get_client (tcp_server_t *server, socket_t socket, void **out_data)
Get client data. void tcp_server_foreach_client (tcp_server_t *server, tcp_client_foreach_fn callback, void *user_arg)
Iterate over all clients. size_t tcp_server_get_client_count (tcp_server_t *server)
Get client count. const char * tcp_client_context_get_ip (const tcp_client_context_t *ctx, char *buf, size_t len)
Get formatted IP address from client context. int tcp_client_context_get_port (const tcp_client_context_t *ctx)
Get port number from client context. void tcp_server_reject_client (socket_t socket, const char *reason)
Reject client connection with reason. asciichat_error_t tcp_server_spawn_thread (tcp_server_t *server, socket_t client_socket, void *(*thread_func)(void *), void *thread_arg, int stop_id, const char *thread_name)
Spawn a worker thread for a client. asciichat_error_t tcp_server_stop_client_threads (tcp_server_t *server, socket_t client_socket)
Stop all threads for a client in stop_id order. asciichat_error_t tcp_server_get_thread_count (tcp_server_t *server, socket_t client_socket, size_t *count)
Get thread count for a client.

Function Documentation

const char * tcp_client_context_get_ip (const tcp_client_context_t * ctx, char * buf, size_t len)

Get formatted IP address from client context. Extracts and formats the client IP address from the connection context. Works for both IPv4 and IPv6 addresses.

Parameters

ctx Client context structure
buf Output buffer for formatted IP string
len Buffer size (recommend INET6_ADDRSTRLEN = 46 bytes)

Returns

Pointer to buf on success, NULL on error

Definition at line 487 of file lib/network/tcp/server.c.

487                                                                                               {
488   if (!ctx) {
489     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "ctx is NULL");
490     return NULL;
491   }
492   if (!buf) {
493     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "buf is NULL");
494     return NULL;
495   }
496   if (len == 0) {
497     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "len is 0");
498     return NULL;
499   }
500 
501   // Determine address family
502   int addr_family = (ctx->addr.ss_family == AF_INET) ? AF_INET : AF_INET6;
503 
504   // Format IP address using existing utility
505   if (format_ip_address(addr_family, (struct sockaddr *)&ctx->addr, buf, len) != 0) {
506     return NULL;
507   }
508 
509   return buf;
510 }




References tcp_client_context_t::addr,
    ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, format_ip_address(), and
    SET_ERRNO.


Referenced by acds_client_handler().






int
  tcp_client_context_get_port (const tcp_client_context_t * ctx)


Get port number from client context. Extracts the client port
    number from the connection context. Works for both IPv4 and IPv6
  addresses.




Parameters


ctx Client context structure


Returns


Port number (host byte order), or -1 on error


Definition at line 512 of file
    lib/network/tcp/server.c.


512                                                                  {
513   if (!ctx) {
514     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "ctx is NULL");
515     return -1;
516   }
517 
518   // Extract port based on address family
519   if (ctx->addr.ss_family == AF_INET) {
520     struct sockaddr_in *addr_in = (struct sockaddr_in *)&ctx->addr;
521     return ntohs(addr_in->sin_port);
522   } else if (ctx->addr.ss_family == AF_INET6) {
523     struct sockaddr_in6 *addr_in6 = (struct sockaddr_in6 *)&ctx->addr;
524     return ntohs(addr_in6->sin6_port);
525   }
526 
527   SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_STATE, "Unknown address family: %d", ctx->addr.ss_family);
528   return -1;
529 }






References tcp_client_context_t::addr,
    ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, ERROR_INVALID_STATE, and
    SET_ERRNO.






asciichat_error_t tcp_server_add_client
  (tcp_server_t * server, socket_t socket, void *
  client_data)


Add client to registry. Thread-safe registration of a connected
    client with arbitrary user data. The client_data pointer is stored as-is
    (caller retains ownership).




Parameters


server Server structure


socket Client socket (used as lookup key)


client_data User-provided client data (can be NULL)


Returns


ASCIICHAT_OK on success, error code on failure


Definition at line 361 of file
    lib/network/tcp/server.c.


361                                                                                                   {
362   if (!server) {
363     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "server is NULL");
364   }
365 
366   if (socket == INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE) {
367     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "socket is invalid");
368   }
369 
370   // Allocate new entry
371   tcp_client_entry_t *entry = SAFE_MALLOC(sizeof(tcp_client_entry_t), tcp_client_entry_t *);
372   if (!entry) {
373     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to allocate client entry");
374   }
375 
376   entry->socket = socket;
377   entry->client_data = client_data;
378 
379   // Create thread pool for this client
380   char pool_name[64];
381   SAFE_SNPRINTF(pool_name, sizeof(pool_name), "client_%d", socket);
382   entry->threads = thread_pool_create(pool_name);
383   if (!entry->threads) {
384     SAFE_FREE(entry);
385     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to create thread pool for client");
386   }
387 
388   // Add to hash table (thread-safe with write lock)
389   rwlock_wrlock(&server->clients_rwlock);
390   HASH_ADD(hh, server->clients, socket, sizeof(socket_t), entry);
391   rwlock_wrunlock(&server->clients_rwlock);
392 
393   log_debug("Added client socket=%d to registry", socket);
394   return ASCIICHAT_OK;
395 }






References ASCIICHAT_OK,
    tcp_client_entry::client_data, tcp_server::clients,
    tcp_server::clients_rwlock, ERROR_INVALID_PARAM,
    ERROR_MEMORY, INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE, log_debug,
    rwlock_wrlock, rwlock_wrunlock, SAFE_FREE,
    SAFE_MALLOC, SAFE_SNPRINTF, SET_ERRNO,
    tcp_client_entry::socket, thread_pool_create(), and
    tcp_client_entry::threads.


Referenced by acds_client_handler(), and
    add_client().






void
  tcp_server_destroy (tcp_server_t * server)


Shutdown TCP server. Closes listen sockets and cleans up
    resources. Does NOT wait for client threads to exit (caller's
    responsibility).




Parameters


server Server structure to clean up


Definition at line 297 of file
    lib/network/tcp/server.c.


297                                               {
298   if (!server) {
299     return;
300   }
301 
302   log_debug("Shutting down TCP server...");
303 
304   // Signal server to stop
305   atomic_store_bool(&server->running, false);
306 
307   // Close listen sockets
308   if (server->listen_socket != INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE) {
309     log_debug("Closing IPv4 listen socket");
310     socket_close(server->listen_socket);
311     server->listen_socket = INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE;
312   }
313 
314   if (server->listen_socket6 != INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE) {
315     log_debug("Closing IPv6 listen socket");
316     socket_close(server->listen_socket6);
317     server->listen_socket6 = INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE;
318   }
319 
320   // Clean up client registry
321   rwlock_wrlock(&server->clients_rwlock);
322 
323   tcp_client_entry_t *entry = NULL, *tmp = NULL;
324   HASH_ITER(hh, server->clients, entry, tmp) {
325     // Call cleanup callback if set
326     if (server->cleanup_fn && entry->client_data) {
327       server->cleanup_fn(entry->client_data);
328     }
329 
330     // Destroy thread pool (stops all threads and frees resources)
331     if (entry->threads) {
332       thread_pool_destroy(entry->threads);
333       entry->threads = NULL;
334     }
335 
336     HASH_DEL(server->clients, entry);
337     SAFE_FREE(entry);
338   }
339   server->clients = NULL;
340 
341   rwlock_wrunlock(&server->clients_rwlock);
342   rwlock_destroy(&server->clients_rwlock);
343 
344   // Note: This function does NOT wait for client threads to exit
345   // Caller is responsible for thread lifecycle management
346 
347   log_debug("TCP server shutdown complete");
348 }






References atomic_store_bool(),
    tcp_server::cleanup_fn, tcp_client_entry::client_data,
    tcp_server::clients, tcp_server::clients_rwlock,
    INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE, tcp_server::listen_socket,
    tcp_server::listen_socket6, log_debug,
    tcp_server::running, rwlock_destroy(), rwlock_wrlock,
    rwlock_wrunlock, SAFE_FREE, socket_close(),
    thread_pool_destroy(), and tcp_client_entry::threads.


Referenced by acds_server_init(),
    acds_server_shutdown(), and server_main().






void
  tcp_server_foreach_client (tcp_server_t * server,
  tcp_client_foreach_fn callback, void * user_arg)


Iterate over all clients. Thread-safe iteration over all connected
    clients. The callback is called once per client while holding the clients
    mutex.




Parameters


server Server structure


callback Function to call for each client


user_arg User argument passed to callback


Definition at line 456 of file
    lib/network/tcp/server.c.


456                                                                                                      {
457   if (!server || !callback) {
458     return;
459   }
460 
461   rwlock_rdlock(&server->clients_rwlock);
462 
463   tcp_client_entry_t *entry, *tmp;
464   HASH_ITER(hh, server->clients, entry, tmp) {
465     callback(entry->socket, entry->client_data, user_arg);
466   }
467 
468   rwlock_rdunlock(&server->clients_rwlock);
469 }






References tcp_client_entry::client_data,
    tcp_server::clients, tcp_server::clients_rwlock,
    rwlock_rdlock, rwlock_rdunlock, and
    tcp_client_entry::socket.


Referenced by signaling_broadcast(),
    signaling_relay_ice(), and signaling_relay_sdp().






asciichat_error_t tcp_server_get_client
  (tcp_server_t * server, socket_t socket, void ** out_data)


Get client data. Thread-safe lookup of client data by socket.




Parameters


server Server structure


socket Client socket to look up


out_data Output pointer for client data (set to NULL if not found)


Returns


ASCIICHAT_OK if found, ERROR_NOT_FOUND if not in
  registry


Definition at line 434 of file
    lib/network/tcp/server.c.


434                                                                                                 {
435   if (!server || !out_data) {
436     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "server or out_data is NULL");
437   }
438 
439   rwlock_rdlock(&server->clients_rwlock);
440 
441   tcp_client_entry_t *entry = NULL;
442   HASH_FIND(hh, server->clients, &socket, sizeof(socket_t), entry);
443 
444   if (!entry) {
445     *out_data = NULL;
446     rwlock_rdunlock(&server->clients_rwlock);
447     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_STATE, "Client socket=%d not in registry", socket);
448   }
449 
450   *out_data = entry->client_data;
451   rwlock_rdunlock(&server->clients_rwlock);
452 
453   return ASCIICHAT_OK;
454 }






References ASCIICHAT_OK,
    tcp_client_entry::client_data, tcp_server::clients,
    tcp_server::clients_rwlock, ERROR_INVALID_PARAM,
    ERROR_INVALID_STATE, rwlock_rdlock, rwlock_rdunlock,
    and SET_ERRNO.






size_t
  tcp_server_get_client_count (tcp_server_t * server)


Get client count. Thread-safe count of connected clients.




Parameters


server Server structure


Returns


Number of clients in registry


Definition at line 471 of file
    lib/network/tcp/server.c.


471                                                          {
472   if (!server) {
473     return 0;
474   }
475 
476   rwlock_rdlock(&server->clients_rwlock);
477   size_t count = HASH_COUNT(server->clients);
478   rwlock_rdunlock(&server->clients_rwlock);
479 
480   return count;
481 }

    tcp_server::clients_rwlock, rwlock_rdlock, and
    rwlock_rdunlock.

    acds_server_shutdown(), and ui_status_gather().






asciichat_error_t tcp_server_get_thread_count
  (tcp_server_t * server, socket_t client_socket, size_t *
  count)


Get thread count for a client. Thread-safe count of worker threads
    spawned for a client.




Parameters


server Server structure


client_socket Client socket to query


count Output pointer for thread count (set to 0 if client not
  found)


Returns


ASCIICHAT_OK on success, ERROR_NOT_FOUND if client not in
  registry


Definition at line 632 of file
    lib/network/tcp/server.c.


632                                                                                                            {
633   if (!server || !count) {
634     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "server or count is NULL");
635   }
636 
637   if (client_socket == INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE) {
638     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "client_socket is invalid");
639   }
640 
641   *count = 0;
642 
643   // Find client entry
644   rwlock_rdlock(&server->clients_rwlock);
645   tcp_client_entry_t *entry = NULL;
646   HASH_FIND(hh, server->clients, &client_socket, sizeof(socket_t), entry);
647 
648   if (!entry) {
649     rwlock_rdunlock(&server->clients_rwlock);
650     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_NOT_FOUND, "Client socket=%d not in registry", client_socket);
651   }
652 
653   // Get thread count from pool
654   if (entry->threads) {
655     *count = thread_pool_get_count(entry->threads);
656   }
657 
658   rwlock_rdunlock(&server->clients_rwlock);
659 
660   return ASCIICHAT_OK;
661 }






References ASCIICHAT_OK, tcp_server::clients,
    tcp_server::clients_rwlock, ERROR_INVALID_PARAM,
    ERROR_NOT_FOUND, INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE, rwlock_rdlock,
    rwlock_rdunlock, SET_ERRNO, thread_pool_get_count(),
    and tcp_client_entry::threads.






asciichat_error_t tcp_server_init (tcp_server_t *
  server, const tcp_server_config_t * config)


Initialize TCP server. Creates and binds TCP sockets according to
    configuration. At least one of IPv4 or IPv6 must be successfully bound.




Parameters


server Server structure to initialize


Config Module Server configuration


Returns


ASCIICHAT_OK on success, error code on failure


Definition at line 104 of file
    lib/network/tcp/server.c.


104                                                                                            {
105   if (!server || !config) {
106     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "server or config is NULL");
107   }
108 
109   // Note: client_handler is optional - some users may use tcp_server just for socket setup
110   // and implement their own accept loop (like ascii-chat server with its cleanup logic)
111 
112   // Initialize server state
113   memset(server, 0, sizeof(*server));
114   server->listen_socket = INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE;
115   server->listen_socket6 = INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE;
116   atomic_store_bool(&server->running, true);
117   server->config = *config; // Copy config
118 
119   // Initialize client registry
120   server->clients = NULL; // uthash starts with NULL
121   server->cleanup_fn = NULL;
122   if (rwlock_init(&server->clients_rwlock, "clients") != 0) {
123     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_THREAD, "Failed to initialize clients read-write lock");
124   }
125 
126   // Determine which IP versions to bind
127   bool should_bind_ipv4 = config->bind_ipv4;
128   bool should_bind_ipv6 = config->bind_ipv6;
129 
130   // Bind IPv4 socket if requested
131   if (should_bind_ipv4) {
132     const char *ipv4_addr = (config->ipv4_address && config->ipv4_address[0] != '\0') ? config->ipv4_address : NULL;
133     server->listen_socket = bind_and_listen(ipv4_addr, AF_INET, config->port);
134 
135     if (server->listen_socket == INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE) {
136       log_warn("Failed to bind IPv4 socket");
137     }
138   }
139 
140   // Bind IPv6 socket if requested
141   if (should_bind_ipv6) {
142     const char *ipv6_addr = (config->ipv6_address && config->ipv6_address[0] != '\0') ? config->ipv6_address : NULL;
143     server->listen_socket6 = bind_and_listen(ipv6_addr, AF_INET6, config->port);
144 
145     if (server->listen_socket6 == INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE) {
146       log_warn("Failed to bind IPv6 socket");
147     }
148   }
149 
150   // Ensure at least one socket bound successfully
151   if (server->listen_socket == INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE && server->listen_socket6 == INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE) {
152     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_NETWORK_BIND, "Failed to bind any sockets (IPv4 and IPv6 both failed)");
153   }
154 
155   /* Register server with named registry, identified by port */
156   char port_name[32];
157   snprintf(port_name, sizeof(port_name), "server:%d", server->config.port);
158   NAMED_REGISTER(server, port_name, "tcp_server", "0x%tx", NULL);
159 
160   /* Register server's sync primitives with hierarchical naming */
161   NAMED_REGISTER_RWLOCK(&server->clients_rwlock, "clients_rwlock", (uintptr_t)(const void *)(server));
162   NAMED_REGISTER_ATOMIC(&server->running, "is_running", (uintptr_t)(const void *)(server));
163 
164   return ASCIICHAT_OK;
165 }






References ASCIICHAT_OK, atomic_store_bool(),
    tcp_server_config_t::bind_ipv4,
    tcp_server_config_t::bind_ipv6, tcp_server::cleanup_fn,
    tcp_server::clients, tcp_server::clients_rwlock,
    tcp_server::config, ERROR_INVALID_PARAM,
    ERROR_NETWORK_BIND, ERROR_THREAD, INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE,
    tcp_server_config_t::ipv4_address,
    tcp_server_config_t::ipv6_address, tcp_server::listen_socket,
    tcp_server::listen_socket6, log_warn, NAMED_REGISTER,
    NAMED_REGISTER_ATOMIC, NAMED_REGISTER_RWLOCK,
    tcp_server_config_t::port, tcp_server::running,
    rwlock_init(), and SET_ERRNO.


Referenced by acds_server_init(), and
  server_main().






void
  tcp_server_reject_client (socket_t socket, const char *
  reason)


Reject client connection with reason. Helper for rejecting clients
    due to rate limits, capacity limits, etc. Logs the rejection reason and
    closes the socket.




Parameters


socket Client socket to close


reason Human-readable rejection reason (for logging)


Definition at line 531 of file
    lib/network/tcp/server.c.


531                                                                    {
532   if (socket == INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE) {
533     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "socket is INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE");
534     return;
535   }
536 
537   log_warn("Rejecting client connection: %s", reason ? reason : "unknown reason");
538   socket_close(socket);
539 }






References ERROR_INVALID_PARAM,
    INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE, log_warn, SET_ERRNO, and
    socket_close().


Referenced by acds_client_handler().






asciichat_error_t tcp_server_remove_client
  (tcp_server_t * server, socket_t socket)


Remove client from registry. Thread-safe removal of a client. If a
    cleanup callback is set, it will be called with the client_data before
    removal.




Parameters


server Server structure


socket Client socket to remove


Returns


ASCIICHAT_OK if removed, ERROR_NOT_FOUND if not in
  registry


Definition at line 397 of file
    lib/network/tcp/server.c.


397                                                                                   {
398   if (!server) {
399     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "server is NULL");
400   }
401 
402   rwlock_wrlock(&server->clients_rwlock);
403 
404   tcp_client_entry_t *entry = NULL;
405   HASH_FIND(hh, server->clients, &socket, sizeof(socket_t), entry);
406 
407   if (!entry) {
408     rwlock_wrunlock(&server->clients_rwlock);
409     // Already removed (e.g., during shutdown) - this is fine
410     log_debug("Client socket=%d already removed from registry", socket);
411     return ASCIICHAT_OK;
412   }
413 
414   // Call cleanup callback if set
415   if (server->cleanup_fn && entry->client_data) {
416     server->cleanup_fn(entry->client_data);
417   }
418 
419   // Destroy thread pool (stops all threads and frees resources)
420   if (entry->threads) {
421     thread_pool_destroy(entry->threads);
422     entry->threads = NULL;
423   }
424 
425   HASH_DEL(server->clients, entry);
426   SAFE_FREE(entry);
427 
428   rwlock_wrunlock(&server->clients_rwlock);
429 
430   log_debug("Removed client socket=%d from registry", socket);
431   return ASCIICHAT_OK;
432 }






References ASCIICHAT_OK, tcp_server::cleanup_fn,
    tcp_client_entry::client_data, tcp_server::clients,
    tcp_server::clients_rwlock, ERROR_INVALID_PARAM,
    log_debug, rwlock_wrlock, rwlock_wrunlock,
    SAFE_FREE, SET_ERRNO, thread_pool_destroy(), and
    tcp_client_entry::threads.


Referenced by acds_client_handler().






asciichat_error_t tcp_server_run (tcp_server_t *
  server)


Run TCP server accept loop. Accepts client connections and spawns
    handler threads. Blocks until server->running is set to false.




Uses select() with timeout to handle dual-stack sockets and allow
    responsive shutdown. If a status_update_fn is configured, it will be called
    periodically on each select() timeout.




Parameters


server Initialized server structure


Returns


ASCIICHAT_OK on success, error code on failure


Definition at line 167 of file
    lib/network/tcp/server.c.


167                                                        {
168   if (!server) {
169     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "server is NULL");
170   }
171 
172   if (!server->config.client_handler) {
173     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM,
174                      "client_handler is required for tcp_server_run() - use custom accept loop if handler is NULL");
175   }
176 
177   log_debug("TCP server starting accept loop...");
178 
179   while (atomic_load_bool(&server->running)) {
180     // Build fd_set for select()
181     fd_set read_fds;
182     socket_fd_zero(&read_fds);
183     socket_t max_fd = 0;
184 
185     // Add IPv4 socket if available
186     if (server->listen_socket != INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE) {
187       socket_fd_set(server->listen_socket, &read_fds);
188       max_fd = server->listen_socket > max_fd ? server->listen_socket : max_fd;
189     }
190 
191     // Add IPv6 socket if available
192     if (server->listen_socket6 != INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE) {
193       socket_fd_set(server->listen_socket6, &read_fds);
194       max_fd = server->listen_socket6 > max_fd ? server->listen_socket6 : max_fd;
195     }
196 
197     // Use timeout from config (defaults to 1 second if not set)
198     // Convert double seconds to tv_sec and tv_usec
199     double timeout_sec_double = server->config.accept_timeout_sec > 0 ? server->config.accept_timeout_sec : 1.0;
200     time_t timeout_sec = (time_t)timeout_sec_double;
201     long timeout_usec = (long)((timeout_sec_double - timeout_sec) * (double)US_PER_SEC_INT);
202     struct timeval timeout = {.tv_sec = timeout_sec, .tv_usec = timeout_usec};
203 
204     int select_result = socket_select((int)(max_fd + 1), &read_fds, NULL, NULL, &timeout);
205 
206     if (select_result < 0) {
207       // Check if interrupted by signal (expected during shutdown)
208       int err = socket_get_last_error();
209       if (err == EINTR) {
210         // Signal interrupt (e.g., SIGTERM, SIGINT) - check running flag and continue
211         log_debug("select() interrupted by signal");
212         continue;
213       }
214       // Actual error - socket_get_error_string() returns last socket error
215       log_error("select() failed in accept loop: %s (errno=%d)", socket_get_error_string(), err);
216       continue;
217     }
218 
219     if (select_result == 0) {
220       // Timeout - invoke status update callback if configured
221       if (server->config.status_update_fn) {
222         server->config.status_update_fn(server->config.status_update_data);
223       }
224       continue;
225     }
226 
227     // Check which socket has incoming connection
228     socket_t ready_socket = INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE;
229     if (server->listen_socket != INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE && socket_fd_isset(server->listen_socket, &read_fds)) {
230       ready_socket = server->listen_socket;
231     } else if (server->listen_socket6 != INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE && socket_fd_isset(server->listen_socket6, &read_fds)) {
232       ready_socket = server->listen_socket6;
233     }
234 
235     if (ready_socket == INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE) {
236       // Spurious wakeup
237       continue;
238     }
239 
240     // Accept connection
241     struct sockaddr_storage client_addr;
242     socklen_t client_addr_len = sizeof(client_addr);
243     socket_t client_socket = accept(ready_socket, (struct sockaddr *)&client_addr, &client_addr_len);
244 
245     if (client_socket == INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE) {
246       log_warn("Failed to accept connection");
247       continue;
248     }
249 
250     // Format client IP for logging
251     char client_ip[INET6_ADDRSTRLEN];
252     // NOLINTNEXTLINE(clang-analyzer-core.UndefinedBinaryOperatorResult) - client_addr filled by accept()
253     int addr_family = (client_addr.ss_family == AF_INET) ? AF_INET : AF_INET6;
254     if (format_ip_address(addr_family, (struct sockaddr *)&client_addr, client_ip, sizeof(client_ip)) != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
255       SAFE_STRNCPY(client_ip, "(unknown)", sizeof(client_ip));
256     }
257 
258     log_debug("Accepted connection from %s", client_ip);
259 
260     // Allocate client context
261     tcp_client_context_t *ctx = SAFE_MALLOC(sizeof(tcp_client_context_t), tcp_client_context_t *);
262     if (!ctx) {
263       log_error("Failed to allocate client context");
264       socket_close(client_socket);
265       continue;
266     }
267 
268     ctx->client_socket = client_socket;
269     ctx->addr = client_addr;
270     ctx->addr_len = client_addr_len;
271     ctx->user_data = server->config.user_data;
272 
273     // Spawn client handler thread
274     // Handler is responsible for:
275     // 1. Allocating client_data
276     // 2. Calling tcp_server_add_client() to register
277     // 3. Spawning additional worker threads via tcp_server_spawn_thread() if needed
278     // 4. Processing client requests
279     // 5. Calling tcp_server_remove_client() on disconnect
280     // 6. Closing socket and freeing ctx
281     asciichat_thread_t thread;
282     if (asciichat_thread_create(&thread, "tcp_client", server->config.client_handler, ctx) != 0) {
283       log_error("Failed to create client handler thread for %s", client_ip);
284       SAFE_FREE(ctx);
285       socket_close(client_socket);
286       continue;
287     }
288 
289     // Thread is detached (handler is responsible for cleanup)
290     (void)thread; // Suppress unused warning
291   }
292 
293   log_debug("TCP server accept loop exited");
294   return ASCIICHAT_OK;
295 }






References tcp_server_config_t::accept_timeout_sec,
    tcp_client_context_t::addr, tcp_client_context_t::addr_len,
    ASCIICHAT_OK, asciichat_thread_create(),
    atomic_load_bool(), tcp_server_config_t::client_handler,
    tcp_client_context_t::client_socket, tcp_server::config,
    EINTR, ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, format_ip_address(),
    INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE, tcp_server::listen_socket,
    tcp_server::listen_socket6, log_debug, log_error,
    log_warn, tcp_server::running, SAFE_FREE,
    SAFE_MALLOC, SAFE_STRNCPY, SET_ERRNO,
    socket_close(), socket_fd_isset(), socket_fd_set(),
    socket_fd_zero(), socket_get_error_string(),
    socket_get_last_error(), socket_select(),
    tcp_server_config_t::status_update_data,
    tcp_server_config_t::status_update_fn, US_PER_SEC_INT,
    tcp_client_context_t::user_data, and
    tcp_server_config_t::user_data.


Referenced by acds_server_run(), and
  server_main().






void
  tcp_server_set_cleanup_callback (tcp_server_t * server,
  tcp_client_cleanup_fn cleanup_fn)


Set client cleanup callback. Sets the callback function that will
    be called when a client is removed from the registry. Use this to free any
    allocated client_data.




Parameters


server Server structure


cleanup_fn Cleanup callback (or NULL to disable)


Definition at line 354 of file
    lib/network/tcp/server.c.


354                                                                                              {
355   if (!server) {
356     return;
357   }
358   server->cleanup_fn = cleanup_fn;
359 }






References tcp_server::cleanup_fn.






asciichat_error_t tcp_server_spawn_thread
  (tcp_server_t * server, socket_t client_socket, void *(*
  thread_func )(void *), void * thread_arg, int stop_id, const char *
  thread_name)


Spawn a worker thread for a client. Creates and tracks a new
    worker thread for the specified client. Threads are identified by stop_id
    for ordered cleanup - lower stop_id values are stopped first when the client
    disconnects.




Example stop_id ordering:


    
  
  • stop_id=1: Receive thread (stop first to prevent new data)
    • 
  
  • stop_id=2: Render threads (stop after receive)
    • 
  
  • stop_id=3: Send thread (stop last after all processing done)
    • 



Parameters


server Server structure


client_socket Client socket to spawn thread for


thread_func Thread function to execute


thread_arg Argument passed to thread function


stop_id Cleanup order (lower = stop first)


thread_name Thread name for debugging (max 63 chars)


Returns


ASCIICHAT_OK on success, error code on failure


Definition at line 545 of file
    lib/network/tcp/server.c.


546                                                                                                   {
547   if (!server || !thread_func) {
548     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "server or thread_func is NULL");
549   }
550 
551   // For WebRTC clients (no socket): create thread directly without TCP server thread pool tracking
552   // This allows render threads to work for both TCP and WebRTC clients
553   if (client_socket == INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE) {
554     // Extract thread handle from thread_arg if it's a client_info_t
555     // We need to store the thread handle somewhere, but for now just create the thread
556     // The caller must manage the thread handle directly for WebRTC clients
557     log_debug("Spawning standalone thread '%s' (no socket, WebRTC client)", thread_name ? thread_name : "unnamed");
558 
559     // For WebRTC clients, we can't use thread_pool since there's no socket entry
560     // Instead, create the thread directly - the caller must handle the thread
561     // This is a hack to allow reusing tcp_server_spawn_thread for WebRTC
562     // In the future, consider a unified thread management system
563     asciichat_thread_t temp_thread;
564     asciichat_error_t result = asciichat_thread_create(&temp_thread, "tcp_worker", thread_func, thread_arg);
565     if (result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
566       return result;
567     }
568 
569     // Note: temp_thread handle is lost here, but the thread is running
570     // The caller must manage thread lifecycle for WebRTC clients differently
571     (void)temp_thread; // Suppress unused warning
572     return ASCIICHAT_OK;
573   }
574 
575   // Find client entry
576   rwlock_rdlock(&server->clients_rwlock);
577   tcp_client_entry_t *entry = NULL;
578   HASH_FIND(hh, server->clients, &client_socket, sizeof(socket_t), entry);
579 
580   if (!entry) {
581     rwlock_rdunlock(&server->clients_rwlock);
582     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_NOT_FOUND, "Client socket=%d not in registry", client_socket);
583   }
584 
585   // Spawn thread in client's thread pool
586   asciichat_error_t result = thread_pool_spawn(entry->threads, thread_func, thread_arg, stop_id, thread_name);
587 
588   rwlock_rdunlock(&server->clients_rwlock);
589 
590   if (result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
591     return result;
592   }
593 
594   size_t thread_count = thread_pool_get_count(entry->threads);
595   log_debug("Spawned thread '%s' (stop_id=%d) for client socket=%d (total_threads=%zu)",
596             thread_name ? thread_name : "unnamed", stop_id, client_socket, thread_count);
597 
598   return ASCIICHAT_OK;
599 }






References ASCIICHAT_OK, asciichat_thread_create(),
    tcp_server::clients, tcp_server::clients_rwlock,
    ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, ERROR_NOT_FOUND,
    INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE, log_debug, rwlock_rdlock,
    rwlock_rdunlock, SET_ERRNO, thread_pool_get_count(),
    thread_pool_spawn(), and tcp_client_entry::threads.


Referenced by create_client_render_threads().






asciichat_error_t tcp_server_stop_client_threads
  (tcp_server_t * server, socket_t client_socket)


Stop all threads for a client in stop_id order. Stops all worker
    threads spawned for the specified client. Threads are stopped in ascending
    stop_id order (lower values first). Joins each thread to ensure it has fully
    exited before proceeding.




Parameters


server Server structure


client_socket Client socket whose threads to stop


Returns


ASCIICHAT_OK on success, error code on failure


Definition at line 601 of file
    lib/network/tcp/server.c.


601                                                                                                {
602   if (!server) {
603     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "server is NULL");
604   }
605 
606   if (client_socket == INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE) {
607     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "client_socket is invalid");
608   }
609 
610   // Find client entry
611   rwlock_rdlock(&server->clients_rwlock);
612   tcp_client_entry_t *entry = NULL;
613   HASH_FIND(hh, server->clients, &client_socket, sizeof(socket_t), entry);
614 
615   if (!entry) {
616     rwlock_rdunlock(&server->clients_rwlock);
617     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_NOT_FOUND, "Client socket=%d not in registry", client_socket);
618   }
619 
620   // Stop all threads in client's thread pool (in stop_id order)
621   asciichat_error_t result = ASCIICHAT_OK;
622   if (entry->threads) {
623     result = thread_pool_stop_all(entry->threads);
624   }
625 
626   rwlock_rdunlock(&server->clients_rwlock);
627 
628   log_debug("All threads stopped for client socket=%d", client_socket);
629   return result;
630 }






References ASCIICHAT_OK, tcp_server::clients,
    tcp_server::clients_rwlock, ERROR_INVALID_PARAM,
    ERROR_NOT_FOUND, INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE, log_debug,
    rwlock_rdlock, rwlock_rdunlock, SET_ERRNO,
    thread_pool_stop_all(), and tcp_client_entry::threads.


Referenced by remove_client().
